rag_rules: Fix latched expression errors by ensuring they appear only in component implementations and reference valid input data ports or event expressions.
Resolve type mismatch errors by ensuring binary expressions on the right side are of type 'bool' and all identifiers are properly declared.
Address 'named thing must be an expression with a type' errors by tracing unresolved references or invalid helper declarations on the same line.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must be placed in valid component scopes: type annexes for assumptions/guarantees, implementation annexes for bindings.
Use only visible AADL features or eq helpers declared in the same annex when writing AGREE clauses.
Ensure type consistency in AGREE declarations: const and eq helpers must match scalar types (int, real, bool) with their expressions.
When generating AGREE guarantees, relate visible outputs to visible inputs and avoid implementation-only subcomponent paths in type annexes.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Stateful equations should use initialized temporal recurrence like `0.0 -> pre(t) + step`.
Branching logic in AGREE should use nested `if ... then ... else` expressions.
Logical operators in AGREE include `and`, `or`, `not(...)`, and implication `=>`.
anti_patterns: Do not place latched expressions in component types or use invalid input references.
Do not mix incompatible types in binary expressions; ensure boolean expressions are properly typed.
Do not reference undeclared identifiers or invalid helpers in AGREE clauses.
